subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Check if Node, npm, and python are installed, preparing for the next step of installing Claude Code

subsections.title: Participants
content: 1. Proficient in using various chain-of-thought strategies, familiar with cutting-edge and popular open/closed-source large model resources, with personal usage experience, summaries, and insights

2. Proficient in NLP classic deep learning models (such as Transformer series, LLaMA series, GLM series, etc.) and related resources (websites, libraries, blogs, etc.); master at least one common deep learning development framework, such as PyTorch; maintain continuous attention and interest in the working principles and latest news of large-scale generative language models (large models) after GPT-3.5

3. Proficient in Python language, able to use basic regular expressions and command line scripts; familiar with NLP basic concepts and classic tasks (classification, matching, sequence labeling, generation, etc.); able to proficiently use common NLP open-source libraries (HanLP, LTP, Jieba, etc.)
4. Proactive attitude, organized communication, curiosity and self-motivation

subsections.title: Input
content: - Input Description: Install Node, npm, and python through command line.
Note: Output may be similar to "v22.21.1"
Note: Output may be similar to "10.9.4"
Note: Output may be similar to "Python 3.11.13"

subsections.title: Output
content: - Output Name: Environment configuration resources ready
- Output Description: Server has been configured with Python environment and Node environment, meeting the requirements for model deployment

subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Install and configure Claude Code through Node

subsections.title: Participants
content: - Role Name: Algorithm Engineer (Frontend Engineer/Backend Engineer)

subsections.title: Input
content: - Input Name: Install and Configure Claude Code
- Input Description: Install and configure Claude Code based on Node environment
# Configure environment variables (using ~/.bashrc as an example, similar for others like ~/.zshrc)
# Startup success confirmation command, enter command line via claude, if you receive a corresponding reply after entering any text without errors, the configuration is complete

subsections.title: Output
content: - Output Name: Available Claude Code service
- Output Description: Generate PRD file for target website through Claude Code

subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Build tavily-mcp service, supporting web search, content reading, and intelligent Q&A capabilities

subsections.title: Participants
content: - Skill Requirements: Familiar with common linux commands and python

subsections.title: Input
content: - Input Name: tavily-mcp service build and start
- Input Description: Use remote MCP server URL to install and start related mcp services
Use remote MCP server URL and Tavily API key:
- Add the following content to .claude.json file
Run the following code to check if the connection is successful:
# Note: If you see something like "tavily-remote-mcp: https://mcp.tavily.com/mcp (HTTP) - ✓ Connected" then it's successful

subsections.title: Output
content: - Output Name: Available tavily web search mcp service
- Output Description: Build and start tavily-mcp service

subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Build metaso-mcp service, supporting web search, content reading, and intelligent Q&A capabilities

subsections.title: Participants
content: - Skill Requirements: Familiar with common linux commands

subsections.title: Input
content: - Input Name: metaso-mcp service build and start
- Input Description: Use remote MCP server URL to install and start related mcp services
claude mcp add -s user -t http search-metaso https://metaso.cn/api/mcp --header "Authorization: Bearer xxx" #Replace xxx with API key
Run the following code to check if the connection is successful:
# Note: If you see something like "search-metaso: https://metaso.cn/api/mcp (HTTP) - ✓ Connected" then it's successful

subsections.title: Output
content: - Output Name: Available metaso web search mcp service
- Output Description: Build and start metaso-mcp service

steps.title: github, huggingface, arxiv-mcp Service Build and Start (Optional)

subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Build github-mcp service, supporting GitHub website search and content reading capabilities
Build huggingface-mcp service, supporting Hugging Face website search and content reading capabilities
Build arxiv-mcp service, supporting arXiv website search and content reading capabilities

subsections.title: Participants
content: - Skill Requirements: Familiar with common linux commands and python

subsections.title: Input
content: - Input Name: github, huggingface, arxiv-mcp service build and start (optional)
- Input Description: Install and start related mcp services by installing python dependencies
# Recommended python 3.10 or above, such as python 3.11
# Note: If you see something like "Uvicorn running on http://0.0.0.0:PORT (Press CTRL+C to quit)" then startup is successful
claude mcp add -s user -t sse paper-arxiv http://127.0.0.1:4002/sse
claude mcp add -s user -t sse code-github http://127.0.0.1:4001/sse
claude mcp add -s user -t sse model-huggingface http://127.0.0.1:4003/sse

subsections.title: Output
content: - Output Name: Available mcp services for GitHub, Hugging Face, and arXiv websites
- Output Description: Build and start github, huggingface, arxiv-mcp services

steps.title: Generate Research Report Using Claude Code

subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Use Claude Code to automatically call the previous MCP services to generate research reports

subsections.title: Participants
content: 1. Proficient in using various chain-of-thought strategies, familiar with cutting-edge and popular open/closed-source large model resources, with personal usage experience, summaries, and insights

2. Proficient in NLP classic deep learning models (such as Transformer series, LLaMA series, GLM series, etc.) and related resources (websites, libraries, blogs, etc.); master at least one common deep learning development framework, such as PyTorch; maintain continuous attention and interest in the working principles and latest news of large-scale generative language models (large models) after GPT-3.5

3. Proficient in Python language, able to use basic regular expressions and command line scripts; familiar with NLP basic concepts and classic tasks (classification, matching, sequence labeling, generation, etc.); able to proficiently use common NLP open-source libraries (HanLP, LTP, Jieba, etc.)
4. Proactive attitude, organized communication, curiosity and self-motivation

subsections.title: Input
content: - Input Name: Generate research report using Claude Code
- Input Description: Interact with Claude Code to confirm input information for generating research videos
Pass in existing research report template.md, research report generation workflow document skill.md, and flowchart.md
└── skill.md # Research report generation workflow document (required)
Start Claude Code in the project root directory to generate research reports
# Start claude, please ensure there are no other files in the project root directory except files, skill.md, and flowchart
> Generate Deerflow research report according to skill.md
# Note: During the process, there will be options that require user confirmation such as research direction, and then the final research report will be automatically generated

subsections.title: Output
content: - Output Description: Claude Code automatically generates the final research report
